behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (collector modules -> summary input assembler), not inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to verify this behavior; in-process fakes for the three source clients are sufficient. LLM-DEP: assertion is on pre-summary payload composition, not model output. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss a real failure where one adapter returns data under a slightly different field name and the assembler silently drops that source. REJECTION-GATE (higher rejected): System is disqualified by behavior text "content passed to the summary-generation step" (checks internal wiring, not real HTTP/subprocess). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior does not require judging model decisions or multi-step agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real assembler/orchestrator modules with fake clients for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log; execute one run; assert the object/text sent into summary-generation contains non-empty contributions from all three source sections with correct source attribution.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on orchestration across modules (source fetch error propagation -> report section fallback), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: can reproduce with in-process failure injection (e.g., one client raises timeout/503 exception), no mandatory real network. LLM-DEP: required output is a literal fallback note ("data unavailable"), which is deterministic script behavior, not model-quality judgment. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) correctness does not depend on real AI output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no planner/tool/critic handoff. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss a real failure where fetch-layer exception type is swallowed incorrectly by orchestrator and whole run aborts. REJECTION-GATE (higher rejected): System disqualified because behavior can be triggered by deterministic local exception injection; Agentic/Workflow disqualified by explicit literal note requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with two healthy fake sources and one fake source configured to fail; assert run completes, report includes the failed section with `data unavailable`, and remaining sections are present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real summary generator produces from inputs (factual preservation/representation), which is model-output quality and can vary run-to-run. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed summary text would always pass format checks while missing real hallucination/omission failures. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration would miss a real failure where the live model drops a critical KPI or flips a trend direction despite correct prompt plumbing. REJECTION-GATE (higher rejected): Workflow disqualified because behavior describes one generation decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use representative real input bundles and call the real summary model; evaluate whether key numeric values/trends from input appear correctly in the narrative (with tolerance rubric) across repeated runs to detect omission/distortion patterns.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this behavior crosses real HTTP/internal APIs plus real email transport/inbox delivery, which are external-system boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email transport can pass while real SMTP/API auth, routing, or provider acceptance fails, so real infrastructure is needed. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration would miss a real failure where provider accepts API call but message never lands in inbox due to delivery pipeline/configuration. REJECTION-GATE (higher rejected): Agentic disqualified because the asserted behavior is delivery to inbox, not model decision quality; Workflow disqualified because no multi-agentic step sequence is required for pass/fail.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled/report job against reachable staging endpoints and real email delivery path to a test mailbox; assert message arrival in the configured inbox with expected subject/attachment/body structure and source-derived sections present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure mode includes real email-service reachability/network behavior plus persistence across actual run boundaries; local in-process mocks can miss transport-level errors and retry-trigger conditions. MOCK-HIDE: fake mail client raising one canned exception may not match real unreachable conditions (connection reset, DNS, TLS, timeout), hiding fallback/retry defects. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration would miss a real failure where the real mail SDK emits a different exception class than the mock, so file fallback is skipped and retry state is not recorded. REJECTION-GATE (higher rejected): Agentic disqualified because behavior is transport fallback/retry, not AI output quality; Workflow disqualified because it is not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run job with real filesystem path and email endpoint intentionally unreachable in a controlled system environment; verify report file is written locally, then run next scheduled execution window (or equivalent scheduler-triggered second run) and verify retry attempts delivery using persisted artifact/state.
